counterfactuals.change: Do not fund Thermalist unless lock is durable
Use one extra high-adjacency city line in final action cycle
Play direct TR card instead of selling in final generation

key_turning_points.title: Rush line did not close by first global max
Thermalist funded but not won
Late board VP conversion underused

rule_updates: If award lock confidence is not high, do not fund it even when tempo feels good.
If first global maxes and finish is not imminent, pivot immediately to VP floor lanes.
In final generation, convert MC to immediate VP before passing whenever legal.

counterfactuals.change: Contest Builder earlier or force opponent off milestone line
Start city-card VP pivot by Gen 6 once rush closure is uncertain
Reduce negative/low-ceiling card mix and increase direct VP density

key_turning_points.title: Opponent discount stack matured
Failed to add second scalable VP engine early
Game pace drifted to full-length engine game

rule_updates: If projected finish is Gen 11 or later by Gen 4, stop single-lane rush and add a second VP engine.
Track opponent card VP ceiling by Gen 6 and pivot if projection exceeds 35 VP.
Treat discount stack completion as a strategic alarm to deny or accelerate closure immediately.

tool_mismatches.actual: Timed out, but action had already executed server-side
tool_mismatches.expected: Sell patents action should return immediately
tool_mismatches.mitigation: After timeout, always poll game state before retrying to avoid duplicate actions.

tool_mismatches.actual: Server rejected without nested space payload
tool_mismatches.expected: Selecting option index should complete greenery placement
tool_mismatches.mitigation: For `or` with `space` branch, always include `sub_response_json` with `type=space` and `spaceId`.

counterfactuals.change: Avoid funding awards unless win projection is durable
Pre-plan fallback VP line when project-card submit fails in endgame
Contest or deny Builder/Planner by midgame before opponent lock

key_turning_points.title: Funded awards without durable lock on Banker/Landlord trajectories
Open City project-card submission failed on server mismatch
Opponent final public-point conversion after Mars completion

rule_updates: If award lock confidence is not durable, do not fund the award and convert actions into direct VP or denial.
If opponent has two live milestone races by Gen 6, make an explicit contest-or-concede decision immediately.
If a project-card submit fails twice in endgame, stop retrying and execute a known fallback VP action path.

tool_mismatches.actual: Rejected with invalid SelectProjectCardToPlayResponse when nested shape used cards instead of card plus full payment
tool_mismatches.expected: Project-card branch accepts nested payload and resolves card play
tool_mismatches.mitigation: Use strict nested projectCard response with card and full payment object including all spendable keys.

tool_mismatches.actual: Rejected with Unknown card name Open City across multiple payload encodings
tool_mismatches.expected: Open City plays normally because it appears in waiting_for project card list
tool_mismatches.mitigation: Treat as known server mismatch and pivot immediately to legal fallback action while logging the mismatch.

counterfactuals.change: Shift to hard closure and engine denial one generation earlier (Gen 8)
Do not fund Banker unless second-place lock is durable
Convert additional MC into immediate board VP before opponent final greenery bursts

key_turning_points.title: Opponent discount and draw stack matured into high card VP ceiling
Opponent secured Scientist and Landlord trajectories while Banker became contested
Late closure started after opponent had already established dominant board/animal conversion

rule_updates: If opponent projected card_vp exceeds 40 by Gen 9, then prioritize closure/denial over economy setup.
If award lock confidence is not high, do not fund and convert actions to direct VP instead.
If oceans and oxygen are near cap by Gen 10, reserve MC for guaranteed temperature/TR closure actions.

tool_mismatches.actual: Pass call timed out while action still executed
tool_mismatches.expected: Pass action returns with immediate next-turn state
tool_mismatches.mitigation: After timeout, poll get_game_state before any retry to avoid duplicate submissions.

counterfactuals.change: Build one additional city anchor by Gen 8 and route greeneries to own city network sooner
Shift one generation earlier from setup cards to direct VP liquidation
Force temperature closure by Gen 10-11 once opponent card-VP ceiling became clear

key_turning_points.title: Public point split locked into Landlord vs Miner race
Hard closure pivot stabilized TR/board floor but did not catch card-VP gap
Opponent city/animal/card stack conversion outscaled our late VP conversion

rule_updates: If opponent projected card_vp is 35 or higher by Gen 8, pivot immediately to forced closure and city-denial lines.
If entering Gen 10+ with 40+ MC and only one global track unfinished, pre-commit to a two-action liquidation plan before passing.
If opponent is likely to lock Banker or Miner by midgame, either commit to contest immediately or ignore and spend actions on guaranteed board/card VP.

counterfactuals.change: Make an explicit Gen 7 contest-or-concede decision on Banker and Miner before John can lock all three awards
Convert one additional midgame action into city-denial/adjacency pressure around John's cluster instead of pure TR tempo
Fund a live award only if first-place hold is credible; otherwise convert that timing window into direct board VP

key_turning_points.title: Terraformer claim plus continued closure pressure prevented a long engine game
Board/card-VP pivot with Domed Crater and Builder kept the game close
Opponent funded Banker, Miner, and later Scientist without meaningful contest

rule_updates: If the opponent has two or more strong award lanes by Gen 7, then make a written contest-or-concede decision before they fund.
If one global is within 3 to 4 steps of completion, then prefer city-and-greenery VP conversion over another generic terraforming action when immediate adjacency exists.
If a late animal or card-VP play does not score within one guaranteed follow-up action, then favor immediate board VP or closure instead.

counterfactuals.change: Treat the opponent's science-discount stack as a hard closure alarm by Gen 8 and force an even faster finish
Convert one additional midgame action into city-denial or board pressure around John's eventual animal cluster
Start a stronger card-VP lane one generation earlier rather than leaning mostly on public points and awards

key_turning_points.title: Big Asteroid plus continued heat conversion bought time against John's plant engine
Ecological Zone and Eos Chasma pivot created a real VP floor instead of pure TR play
John's Anti-Gravity Technology and Space Station completed the discount stack before his final animal burst

rule_updates: If the opponent completes a science-discount stack by Gen 8, then immediately switch to maximum closure mode unless our own card-VP ceiling is already competitive.
If our win plan is public-points heavy, then require at least one additional scalable card-VP lane by Gen 7 instead of trusting TR plus awards to hold.
If temperature and oceans are already closed or nearly closed, then value oxygen-ending greenery lines above almost all remaining setup cards unless a card scores immediately this generation.

counterfactuals.change: After finishing Mars in Gen 11, continue the rest of the generation and convert MC into one more standard city plus follow-up greenery instead of passing
Treat AI Central plus Mass Converter plus Quantum Extractor as a hard closure emergency by Gen 8 and reserve more Gen 9-10 cash for guaranteed closing actions
Concede Banker earlier once John overtook the category and redirect one award/conversion window into direct board VP

key_turning_points.title: Pivoted into Zeppelins and board conversion, but John's discount stack still matured
John funded Miner and overtook Banker while his late-game card engine remained intact
Passed after terraforming instead of liquidating the rest of the generation into board VP

rule_updates: If Mars becomes fully terraformed during the action phase, then assume the rest of the generation is still live until the prompt proves otherwise and liquidate all remaining MC/plants/steel before passing.
If John has AI Central plus Mass Converter and adds another science or space discount by Gen 8, then reserve Gen 9-10 actions primarily for guaranteed closure and board VP, not additional setup.
If a funded award trajectory flips by Gen 10, then stop defending sunk-cost pride and convert the remaining windows into guaranteed points instead of leaving the category to drift away.

tool_mismatches.actual: Treated post-terraform state as if only final greenery cleanup remained and passed with large MC and steel stranded
tool_mismatches.expected: After Mars is terraformed, continue taking remaining actions in the generation until no profitable conversions remain
tool_mismatches.mitigation: After any terraforming-complete action, explicitly verify whether the generation is still live and exhaust all positive-EV conversions before passing

counterfactuals.change: Force a hard-close from Generation 8 once the Saturn discount and draw stack is clearly ahead on final ceiling
Do not fund Scientist unless the lead is durable against the opponent's visible science follow-up
Convert stranded steel and MC into board VP earlier instead of preserving optionality

key_turning_points.title: John's Saturn discount and draw engine was already beyond a fair late-game race, but closure pressure stayed too soft
Late Jovian, animal, and card-draw scaling created an unrecoverable card-VP gap
Even with better board play and public points, too much late steel and MC still failed to convert into maximum VP-per-action

rule_updates: If a Saturn or equivalent titanium-discount engine has strong draw and large MC production by Gen 7, then switch to hard-close mode immediately.
If a funded award can realistically flip under the opponent's visible engine path, then do not fund it.
If stranded steel exceeds one good building play in the late midgame, then prioritize board-scoring buildings or standard projects before taking more setup cards.

tool_mismatches.actual: Some non-building cards rejected mixed steel and MC payments with HTTP 400 errors
tool_mismatches.expected: Steel payments should only be used when the server clearly allows them for that card
tool_mismatches.mitigation: Default to MC-only unless the card is clearly steel-payable and the server has already accepted that pattern
